---
title: "combine_forecast_obs_WOW"
author: "Wenkai_Zhong"
date: "2025-07-25"
output: html_document
---

############################### before run this file u should run three individual data import files first (forecast,obs,wow)#######################################
```{r}
# Set working directory if needed
# setwd(here())
```

#combine forecast with obs
```{r}
combine_forecast_obs_wow <- function(date_str, 
                               gridpoint_id, 
                               lead_time_hours = NULL,
                               aggregate_obs = "hourly",
                               include_metadata = TRUE) {
 
 # Get forecast data for the specified date and gridpoint
 forecast_data <- tidy_forecast_with_station_grid_location(date_str, gridpoint_id)
 
 ####### Import observation data for 10 days starting from date_str
 # Convert date_str to Date object
 start_date <- as.Date(date_str)
 
 # Initialize list to store observation data
 all_obs_data <- list()
 
 # Loop through 10 days
 for (i in 0:9) {
   current_date <- start_date + i
   current_date_str <- format(current_date, "%Y-%m-%d")
   
   tryCatch({
     # Import observation data for current date
     daily_obs <- import_knmi_data(current_date_str)
     all_obs_data[[current_date_str]] <- daily_obs
   })
 }
 
 # Combine all observation data
 if (length(all_obs_data) > 0) {
   obs_data <- bind_rows(all_obs_data)
 } else {
   warning("No observation data found for any date")
   return(forecast_data)
 }
 
 # Extract station_id from forecast data
 station_id <- unique(forecast_data$station_id)
 
 # Check if station_id exists
 if(is.na(station_id) || length(station_id) == 0) {
   warning("No station_id found for this gridpoint")
   return(forecast_data)
 }
 
 # Filter observation data for the specific station
 pattern <- paste0("^", station_id, "_")
 
 obs_filtered <- obs_data %>%
   filter(grepl(pattern, LOCATION)) %>%
   # Convert DTG to POSIXct to match forecast data format
   mutate(DTG = as.POSIXct(DTG, tz = "UTC"))
 
 # Check if any observations found
 if(nrow(obs_filtered) == 0) {
   warning(paste("No observations found for station", station_id))
   return(forecast_data)
 }
 
 # Ensure Valid_datetime is POSIXct
 forecast_data$Valid_datetime <- as.POSIXct(forecast_data$Valid_datetime, tz = "UTC")
 
 # Join observation data to forecast data
 combined_data <- forecast_data %>%
   left_join(
     obs_filtered,
     by = c("Valid_datetime" = "DTG"),
     suffix = c("_forecast", "_obs")
   )
 
 return(combined_data)
}

# Usage example
combined <- combine_forecast_obs_wow("2021-01-31 00:00:00", 605)
combined
```

#Combined visualisation funtion
```{r}
forecast_add_obs_line <- function(date_str, gridpoint_id, variable = "Temperature_C", 
                                  members = 1:51, data_dir = here("data", "ToSend-01-20-2025")) {
  
  # Variable mapping for observations
  var_mapping <- list(
    "Temperature_C" = "T_DRYB_10",
    "DewPoint_C" = "T_DEWP_10"
  )
  
  obs_var_name <- var_mapping[[variable]]
  if (is.null(obs_var_name)) {
    stop(paste("Invalid variable:", variable, 
               ". Valid options are:", paste(names(var_mapping), collapse = ", ")))
  }
  
  # Get the combined forecast and observation data
  combined <- combine_forecast_obs_wow(date_str, gridpoint_id)
  
  if (is.null(combined) || nrow(combined) == 0) {
    warning(paste("No combined data found for", date_str, "at gridpoint", gridpoint_id))
    return(NULL)
  }
  
  # Filter for selected members
  member_names <- paste0("M", members)
  plot_data <- combined %>%
    filter(Member %in% member_names) %>%
    mutate(
      Forecast_time = format(as.POSIXct(Forecast_init_datetime), "%H:%M"),
      Lead_time_hours = as.numeric(lead_time_hrs)
    )
  
  # Create the plot
  p <- ggplot(plot_data, aes(x = Lead_time_hours))
  
  # Check number of members
  n_members <- length(members)
  
  # Add forecast lines
  if (n_members <= 10) {
    # Use colors that don't include red (reserved for observations)
    forecast_colors <- c("blue", "darkgreen", "purple", "orange", "brown", 
                        "pink", "gold", "cyan", "magenta", "gray")[1:n_members]
    p <- p + 
      geom_line(aes(y = .data[[variable]], color = Member, group = Member), 
                size = 1.2, alpha = 0.8) +
      scale_color_manual(
        name = "Ensemble",
        values = forecast_colors
      )
  } else {
    p <- p + 
      geom_line(aes(y = .data[[variable]], group = Member), 
                alpha = 0.6, color = "gray", size = 0.8)
  }
  
  # Add observation line
  p <- p + 
    geom_line(aes(y = .data[[obs_var_name]]), 
              color = "red", 
              size = 2, 
              alpha = 1) +
    geom_point(aes(y = .data[[obs_var_name]]), 
               color = "darkred", 
               size = 2, 
               alpha = 0.8)
  
  # Add faceting by forecast initialization time
  p <- p +
    facet_wrap(~ Forecast_time, ncol = 1) +
    labs(
      title = paste("Forecast made on", date_str, "at Gridpoint", gridpoint_id),
      subtitle = paste(n_members, "ensemble members | Red: Observations (", obs_var_name, ")"),
      x = "Lead Time (hours)",
      y = ifelse(variable == "Temperature_C", "Temperature (°C)", 
                 ifelse(variable == "DewPoint_C", "Dew Point (°C)", variable))
    ) +
    theme_minimal() +
    theme(
      plot.title = element_text(size = 14, face = "bold"),
      plot.subtitle = element_text(size = 12),
      legend.position = if(n_members <= 10) "right" else "none",
      panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
      strip.text = element_text(size = 11, face = "bold")
    ) +
    scale_x_continuous(breaks = seq(0, 240, 24)) +
    coord_cartesian(xlim = c(0, 240))
  
  return(p)
}

# Usage
p <- forecast_add_obs_line("2021-01-31 00:00:00", 605, variable = "Temperature_C")
print(p)



```
